steps.title: Identify the structural requirement
steps.content: After the appositive phrase in dashes, the subject “Several advantages” requires a verb to form an independent clause before the semicolon.

steps.title: Apply the finite verb rule
steps.content:
An independent clause requires a finite verb conjugated for person and tense. Have positioned is the only finite verb here, and it correctly agrees with the plural subject “advantages” while taking “acoustic waves” as its object.

The other three choices — “having positioned,” “by positioning,” and “positioning” — are all non-finite forms that cannot serve as the main verb of an independent clause.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
